data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness criticism's strict advantage over self-consistency emerges primarily at odd k >= 7 when the wrong-answer concentration is thin (a bare 4-of-7 majority) and the critic possesses high precision on exactly those bare-majority wrong sets, because self-consistency's tie-breaking resolves noise while the critic removes a structurally identifiable wrong cluster.","mechanism": "At k=5 the majority margin is at least 3-vs-2, giving self-consistency a weak but decisive signal; when the pool is already right 4-of-5 (as in the repository's hard set) filtering is redundant and when it is wrong 3-of-2 the critic has too little signal to distinguish a wrong majority from a right one with the observed precision 0.125. Increasing k to 7 creates a richer margin structure: bare 4-of-7 wrong majorities carry distinctive candidate-set features (near-tie entropy, higher variance in reasoning quality among the 4) that a critic can learn to flag at higher precision than the blunt k=5 case. The critic thus converts close-call noise that self-consistency would lock in as a wrong mode into a rerun or abstention, strictly improving accuracy when bare-majority wrong sets are common (base error ~0.55-0.65) and critic precision on bare-majority sets exceeds ~0.7.","scope": {"covers": ["k=7 or k=9", "base error 0.50-0.70 (thin wrong majorities)", "critic precision on bare-majority wrong sets >= 0.7"], "excludes": ["k=5 (margin structure too coarse)", "base error < 0.40 (robust right majorities)", "base error > 0.75 (robust wrong majorities, critic cannot rescue even at high precision)"]},"forbidden": [{"case": "At k=7 with base error 0.55-0.65 and critic precision on bare-majority wrong sets >= 0.7, harness accuracy is not strictly greater than self-consistency on >= 60% of questions in that bin", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At k=7 with base error 0.55-0.65, critic precision on bare-majority wrong sets < 0.4, and harness accuracy still exceeds self-consistency on >= 50% of questions", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "The improvement at k=7 is fully explained by base-error change alone with no interaction effect from critic precision on bare-majority sets", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}],"prose_notes": "Experiment: 100 questions at base error ~0.60, generate pools at k=5, k=7, k=9 (same questions, same seeds, just more samples); compute per-k self-consistency accuracy and harness accuracy; measure critic precision/recall stratified by margin (bare-majority vs robust-majority). Test for interaction: harness-SC gap should grow with k only when critic precision on bare-majority wrong sets is high. Token budget: 100 q * 9 cand * 800 tokens + 100 q * 3 critic runs * 600 tokens + analysis ~= 120k."}
